account.danger.delete_account_body: This clears your projects, topics, decisions, and Q&A history from Inspira. It can't be undone.
account.danger.error: Couldn't delete the account. Please try again.
account.danger.heading: Danger zone
account.danger.subtitle: Irreversible destinations. Take a breath first.
account.danger.system_note: You're using a shared session, so there's no personal account to delete. Sign in to your own account first.
account.deactivated.body: It was closed on {deleted_at}. Your projects, topics, decisions, and Q&A are gone — we mean that when we say 'deleted.'
account.deactivated.heading: This account has been deleted.
account.deactivated.subtext: Want to start fresh? You can create a new account with the same email any time.
account.email_prefs.footer_note: You can also unsubscribe from any email's footer.
account.email_prefs.heading: Email preferences
account.email_prefs.load_error: Couldn't load your email preferences right now.
account.email_prefs.save_error: Couldn't save that change. Please try again.
account.email_prefs.security_always_on: Security emails always send. They protect your account.
account.email_prefs.subtitle: Which emails you'd like from us, if any.
account.email_prefs.unavailable: Coming soon — email preferences land with the next backend release.
account.locale_toggle_note: Future planner output will be in your new language. Existing content stays as-is.
account.members.link_help: Invite teammates, manage roles, and see who has a seat.
account.page.heading: Account.
account.password.error_generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.
account.password.error_mismatch: The two new-password fields don't match.
account.password.error_rejected: The server rejected that password — try a longer or stronger one.
account.password.error_same: New password must be different from the current one.
account.password.error_too_short: New password must be at least 8 characters.
account.password.heading: Password
account.password.subtitle: Use something long and unique. Minimum 8 characters.
account.password.subtitle_system: Sign in to an account first before changing a password.
account.profile.email_hint: Your account identifier; change requires support.
account.profile.heading: Profile
account.profile.save_error: Couldn't save that right now. Please try again.
account.profile.subtitle: The name we greet you with. Your email stays as your sign-in identifier.
account.reset_tours_help: See the quick guided tours again on each screen.
account.security.disable.body: You'll sign in with just your password. You can turn it back on any time from Security settings.
account.security.disable.error_generic: Something went wrong. Check your password and try again.
account.security.disable.success_body: We removed your authenticator and invalidated your recovery codes. You can turn two-factor back on any time from Security settings.
account.security.disable.title: Turn off two-factor authentication?
account.security.heading: Security
account.security.sessions_error: Couldn't load sessions right now. Try again in a moment.
account.security.sessions_help: Devices signed into this account right now.
account.security.subtitle: Two-factor authentication and active sign-ins.
account.security.unavailable: Coming soon — sign-in controls land with the next backend release.
account.theme.heading: Theme
account.theme.subtitle: Choose your style and color mode. Applies immediately.
account.twofa_disable.body: Turning off two-factor lowers your account protection. Type your password to confirm.
account.twofa_disable.error: Couldn't turn it off. Check your password and try again.
account.twofa_disable.title: Turn off two-factor
account.twofa_setup.step1_body: Open your authenticator app and scan the QR. If the camera isn't an option, enter the secret by hand instead.
account.twofa_setup.step2_body: Type the current code from your authenticator so we can confirm the pairing.
account.twofa_setup.step3_body: Keep these somewhere safe. They're the only way back in if you lose your phone.
account.twofa_setup.title: Turn on two-factor
account.twofa_setup.verify_error: That code didn't match. Try the next one from the app.
activity.empty: Nothing yet — once you start mapping, the history shows up here.
activity.error: Couldn't load the activity feed. Try again in a moment.
activity.panel.heading: Activity
api_tokens.copy_copy_failed: Select the token and copy manually.
api_tokens.copy_once_body: This is the only time we show it. Paste it into your script or connector now — we only keep a hash on our side.
api_tokens.create_dialog_body: Give this token a label so you can recognise it later.
api_tokens.create_error: Couldn't create the token. Please try again.
api_tokens.empty: No tokens yet. Create one to let an external script or connector act on your behalf.
api_tokens.heading: API tokens
api_tokens.load_error: Couldn't load your tokens. Please try again.
api_tokens.revoke_error: Couldn't revoke that token. Please try again.
api_tokens.subtitle: Give scripts and connectors authenticated access to your canvases. Tokens show once — copy immediately.
api_tokens.system_note: Sign in to an account first before creating API tokens.
auth.email_hint_invalid: That doesn't look like a valid email yet.
auth.email_in_use: That email is already registered. Try signing in instead.
auth.forgot_dev_hint: Email delivery isn’t configured yet. Ask an admin to run `python scripts/reset_password_link.py --email <your-email>` for a direct link.
auth.forgot_generic_error: Couldn't send the reset email. Try again in a moment.
auth.forgot_subtitle: Drop in your email. If an account exists, we'll send a reset link.
auth.forgot_success_body: If an account exists with that address, the reset link is on its way.
auth.forgot_support_fallback: If you don't get an email, contact support — an admin can issue a reset link directly.
auth.generic_error: Something went wrong. Please try again.
auth.invalid_credentials: Email or password didn't match. Try again.
auth.network_error: Couldn't reach Inspira. Check your connection and try again.
auth.terms_required: You must accept the Terms and Privacy Policy to create an account.
auth.password_strength_short: A few more characters — aim for at least 8.
auth.password_strength_weak: Weak — try mixing letters and numbers.
auth.password_too_short: Password must be at least 8 characters.
auth.rate_limited: Too many attempts. Wait a minute and try again.
auth.server_error: Inspira hit a snag on our end. Try again in a moment.
auth.signup_bad_request: Couldn't create the account — double-check your email and password and try again.
auth.subtitle_signup: Keep your own thinking space, private to you.
auth_gate.headline: Create a free account to open your map
auth_gate.lede: Inspira saved what you wrote. Sign in or start an account and we'll finish mapping it into a canvas you can keep.
banner.anonymous.body: Save it to come back later, or open it from a second device.
banner.anonymous.signup_context_export: Create an account to export this canvas.
banner.anonymous.signup_context_new_proj: Sign in or create an account to start a second project.
banner.anonymous.signup_context_share: Create an account to share this canvas with someone.
banner.anonymous.tagline: Your canvas lives in this browser.
billing.account_settings.link_help: Plan, payment method, and invoices live on their own page.
billing.banner.dunning: Payment failed on {date}. Update your payment method within 7 days to restore access.
billing.banner.dunning_note: The canvas stays visible while billing is resolved — edits pause until the card is updated.
billing.banner.grace_deletion: Workspace scheduled for deletion on {date}. Restore within {days} days to keep everything.
billing.banner.suspended: Paid access is paused. Workspace is read-only until billing is updated.
billing.banner.trial_expired: Your {plan} trial ended on {date}. You’re on Free for the next {days} days.
billing.cancel.entry.body: Your workspace stays on Paid until the end of the current period on {date}. After that it drops to Free and any Pro-only data goes read-only while you decide.
billing.cancel.entry.title: Cancel your {plan} plan?
billing.cancel.feedback_label: Anything you’d like us to hear?
billing.cancel.success.body: You’ll stay on {plan} until {date}. Nothing else changes today. Come back any time.
billing.cancel.success.title: Your plan is canceled.
billing.cancel.survey.body: Why are you canceling? Optional, but it helps us make Inspira better for the people staying.
billing.cancel.survey.title: One question before you go.
billing.checkout.accept_tos: I agree to the Terms of Service and Billing Terms.
billing.checkout.charged_note: Charged to {card}. Next renewal {date}. Cancel any time.
billing.checkout.continue_body: Payment happens on Stripe’s secure page. You’ll come back to Inspira when you’re done.
billing.checkout.fallback_body: Billing isn’t turned on for this deployment. Write to {email} and we’ll sort it out.
billing.checkout.refund_note: Refunds: case by case, handled by a human.
billing.checkout.title: {plan} · {period}.
billing.checkout_return.canceled.body: You backed out before completing checkout. Nothing changed on your plan.
billing.checkout_return.canceled.title: No charge.
billing.checkout_return.success.body: Your plan is active. Charges will show up under Recent invoices shortly.
billing.checkout_return.success.title: Welcome to {plan}.
billing.cmp.heading: Feature
billing.delete.body: Everything — projects, topics, decisions, shared links — is scheduled for deletion in {days} days. You can restore any time before then from this same screen.
billing.delete.grace.body: Deleted on {date} ({days} days remaining). Restore any time before then.
billing.delete.grace.title: Workspace scheduled for deletion.
billing.delete.title: Delete this workspace?
billing.fallback.not_configured_body: Reach out at {email} if you’d like an invitation.
billing.fallback.not_configured_title: Billing isn’t active on this deployment yet.
billing.footer.refund_note: Refunds are case by case, handled by a human.
billing.hero.sub.active_paid_annual: Annual plan, renews {date}. {amount} billed once a year.
billing.hero.sub.free: Free forever. Upgrade when a project outgrows three slots or you want company on the canvas.
billing.hero.sub.past_due: Payment failed {date}. Update your card within 7 days and nothing else changes.
billing.hero.sub.suspended: Workspace is read-only. Your projects are safe — nothing is deleted.
billing.hero.sub.trial_1d: Upgrade today and nothing changes. If not, you’ll drop to Free with a short grace period.
billing.hero.sub.trial_3d: No interruption if you upgrade before {date}.
billing.hero.sub.trial_expired_grace: Trial ended {date}. You have {days} days before Free limits apply to your projects.
billing.hero.sub.trialing: {days} days remaining. Becomes {price} when the trial ends.
billing.hero.title.past_due: There’s a hiccup with your card.
billing.hero.title.trial_3d: Three days left on your trial.
billing.invoice_detail.pdf_unavailable: PDF will be available once the backend lands the download endpoint.
billing.invoice_detail.title: Invoice {number}
billing.invoices.empty: No invoices yet — they show up here after the first paid cycle.
billing.invoices_page.empty_body: They land here automatically after the first paid cycle.
billing.invoices_page.title: Invoices
billing.meta.description: Manage your plan, payment method, and invoices.
billing.meta.title: Billing
billing.page.heading: Billing
billing.plans.free.name: Free
billing.plans.free.tagline: For one idea at a time.
billing.plans.heading: Choose your plan.
billing.plans.pro.name: Pro
billing.plans.pro.tagline: Room for every idea you have.
billing.plans.sub: Annual is cheaper. Switch any time.
billing.plans.team.bullet_2: Top-tier reasoning model on every turn (gpt-5.5)
billing.plans.team.bullet_4: Up to 100 business plans per month*
billing.plans.team.name: Frontier
billing.pm.admin_note: Only the billing owner can see or change payment details.
billing.pm.member_note: Members see the plan name and current usage. Reach out to your admin if you need more room.
billing.pm_modal.card_note: Card changes happen on Stripe’s secure page.
billing.pm_modal.remove_confirm_body: Your plan will downgrade to Free at the end of the current period. Paid features stay available until then.
billing.pm_modal.title: Payment method
billing.pm_modal.vat_emails_help: Comma-separated. Receipts go to each address listed here.
billing.quota.audit_log_export.body: CSV and JSON audit exports come with Frontier.
billing.quota.audit_log_export.headline: Keep your own records.
billing.quota.audit_log_export.limit: Audit log export is a Frontier feature
billing.quota.cross_project.body: Pro checks for conflicts across every project, not just within one.
billing.quota.cross_project.headline: See the whole picture.
billing.quota.cross_project.limit: Cross-project checks are a Pro feature
billing.quota.custom_templates.body: Templates travel across projects on Pro.
billing.quota.custom_templates.headline: Save a shape you’ll want again.
billing.quota.custom_templates.limit: Custom templates are a Pro feature
billing.quota.fourth_project.body: You’ve got 3 projects on Free. Pro keeps the canvas open for as many ideas as you have.
billing.quota.fourth_project.headline: Room for every idea.
billing.quota.member_note: Members see Contact billing owner instead of the upgrade button.
billing.quota.multi_reviewer.body: Frontier lets you require multiple reviewers before export.
billing.quota.multi_reviewer.headline: Require more than one set of eyes.
billing.quota.multi_reviewer.limit: Multi-reviewer approvals are a Frontier feature
billing.quota.second_repo.body: Pro unlocks 5 repo connections. Frontier raises that to 25.
billing.quota.second_repo.headline: Connect the repos you actually work in.
billing.quota.third_paid_member.body: Pro adds more collaborators without changing how your canvas feels.
billing.quota.third_paid_member.headline: Bring more of the team in.
billing.tier3.annual.body: Annual is $44 a month instead of $49. Save ~10%. Switch any time.
billing.tier3.annual.title: Save with annual billing.
billing.tier3.refund.amount_hint: Defaults to the full invoice. Edit for a partial refund.
billing.tier3.refund.description_placeho: A couple of sentences is plenty.
billing.tier3.refund.error_amount: Please enter a valid dollar amount.
billing.tier3.refund.error_submit: We couldn’t send the request. Try again in a moment.
billing.tier3.refund.note: Refunds are handled by a person, not a bot. We read every request ourselves and reply within two business days.
billing.tier3.refund.success_body: We read every refund request ourselves. You’ll hear back within two business days.
billing.tier3.refund.title: Request a refund.
billing.tier3.transfer.body: Ownership moves fully. You can transfer it back any time from Billing. The new owner sees payment details, downloads invoices, and manages the plan; you keep every other admin power.
billing.tier3.transfer.error_submit: We couldn’t complete the transfer. Try again in a moment.
billing.tier3.transfer.no_candidates: There’s no one else to hand ownership to yet. Invite an admin from the Members page first.
billing.tier3.transfer.success_body: They’ll get an email with the handoff. You keep every admin power except the billing ones.
billing.tier3.transfer.title: Transfer billing ownership.
byok.footer_disclaimer: We encrypt your key at rest and only use it for requests you initiate. You can remove it at any time.
byok.load_failed: Couldn't load your current BYOK state.
byok.mode_badge_house_key_aria: This turn is billed to Inspira credits.
byok.mode_badge_house_key_title: Running on Inspira's shared API key.
byok.mode_badge_your_key_aria: This turn is billed directly to your provider account.
byok.mode_badge_your_key_title: Running on the API key you saved in Account Settings.
byok.remove_failed: Couldn't remove the key. Try again?
byok.remove_success: Removed. You're back on the house key.
byok.save_failed: Couldn't save your key. Try again?
byok.save_rejected: That key didn't authenticate with the provider.
byok.save_success: Key verified. Future turns will use your account.
byok.section_subtitle: Skip our credit system and bill OpenAI or Anthropic directly. Your key never leaves our server unencrypted.
canvas.actions.activity_title: Show recent activity on this canvas
canvas.actions.summary_title: Open the plan summary + planner views
canvas.composer.add_link_aria: Attach a URL as a source
canvas.composer.add_link_title: Attach a URL as a source
canvas.composer.link_bad_url: Please provide a full URL starting with http:// or https://.
canvas.composer.link_prompt: Paste a URL to attach as a source:
canvas.composer.paste_confirm: This looks like a long block of text ({chars} chars). Attach it as a source instead of pasting into the composer?
canvas.composer.placeholder: New idea, question, or context — drop it here…
canvas.drafting_banner.count_many: Inspira is drafting · {count} topics so far
canvas.drafting_banner.count_one: Inspira is drafting · 1 topic so far
canvas.duplicate.toast_failed: Couldn’t duplicate topic. Try again in a moment.
canvas.duplicate.toast_none_selected: Select a topic first, then press Cmd/Ctrl+D to duplicate it.
canvas.empty.body: Topics will appear here as they’re ready. Hang tight — first pass usually takes about a minute.
canvas.empty.title: Drafting your topics…
canvas.router.context_note: Filed under {title}. Open the topic to review.
canvas.router.error: Couldn’t add that to the canvas. {message}
canvas.router.new_topic_no_connections: Added topic: {title}. Inspira didn’t draw any connections yet.
canvas.summary_button_title: View the summary, outline, and duplicate-topic proposals
canvas.topic_list.aria: Topics list (accessible alternative to the visual canvas)
canvas.topic_list.heading: Topics on this canvas
canvas.topic_list.intro: The canvas above is a visual graph. Use this list to open any topic with the keyboard.
canvas_error.body: We've logged it. Try reloading the canvas, or head back to your projects.
canvas_error.heading: This canvas hit a snag.
canvas_onboard.1.body: Each topic was AI-generated from your idea. Click one to review the planner's questions and decisions.
canvas_onboard.1.title: These are AI-generated topics.
canvas_onboard.2.body: The planner already drafted Q&A and decisions for every topic. Edit anything you want to change — it propagates to the canvas.
canvas_onboard.2.title: Review and edit, don't start from scratch.
canvas_onboard.3.body: Summary turns everything on this canvas into a readable brief you can export.
canvas_onboard.3.title: See the whole picture.
canvas_onboard.4.body: Tidy auto-lays out your topics so the connections read clearly.
canvas_onboard.4.title: Keep it organized.
canvas_onboard.5.body: You can bounce between projects any time. Your canvases stay where you left them.
canvas_onboard.5.title: Back to your projects.
canvas_onboard_topic.1.body: The planner already drafted answers and decisions for this topic. Review each one — edit anything that needs your perspective.
canvas_onboard_topic.1.title: Review the planner's draft.
canvas_onboard_topic.2.body: Type a response below to push back, add context, or take the conversation deeper. The planner picks up from there.
canvas_onboard_topic.2.title: Push back when you need to.
canvas_onboard_topic.3.body: Decisions surface across all topic cards too. Close the panel to see how this topic connects to the rest of your canvas.
canvas_onboard_topic.3.title: Decisions ripple across the canvas.
checkpoints.heading: Progress · {answered} of {total}
command_palette.no_matches_hint: Try a different verb — like “new”, “open”, or “export”.
cookie_banner.body: We use cookies to keep you signed in and to measure how many people find us.
dedupe_view.empty_hint: Your topics each cover their own ground — nothing to merge right now.
dedupe_view.error_body: The planner couldn't finish the dedupe scan. {message}
dedupe_view.keep_both: Keep both, but note the overlap
dedupe_view.loading_status: The planner is scanning for duplicates…
dedupe_view.title: Duplicates
duplicate_dialog.title: Possible duplicate
duplicate_dialog.body_hint: These two topics look similar. What should we do?
delete_dialog.title: Delete {item_type}?
delete_suggestion_dialog.title: Close this topic?
email_confirm.expired_body: Confirmation links are good for 24 hours. Ask for a fresh one and we'll send it now.
email_confirm.loading_body: One moment — we're checking your link.
email_confirm.resend_error: Couldn't send right now. Try again in a moment.
email_confirm.resend_success: Sent. Check your email in a minute.
email_confirm.resend_throttled: We sent one recently. Try again in about {minutes} minutes.
email_confirm.success_body: You're all set. Taking you back to your canvas now.
empty.decisions.body: As you answer questions, the planner will propose decisions you can accept.
empty.decisions.headline: No decisions yet.
empty.projects.body: A project starts as a single sentence — a question, a tension, a thing you want to think through.
empty.projects.headline: No projects yet.
empty.search.body: Try a different word, or fewer words. Search looks at project titles, topic titles, and decision text.
empty.search.headline: Nothing matches “{query}”.
empty.shelves.body: Shelves are quiet groupings — "client work", "personal", "someday/maybe". Make one when the pile gets too tall to scan.
empty.shelves.headline: No shelves yet.
error.not_found_body: This corner of Inspira doesn't exist — or it was moved.
error.not_found_body_with_path: This corner of Inspira doesn't exist — or it was moved (you were looking for {path}).
error.server_default_body: The server stumbled. We've logged it. Try again in a moment, or come back later.
error.session_expired_body: Your session expired. Sign back in to pick up where you left off.
error_boundary.body: An unexpected error occurred while rendering this view. Your work so far is still saved. Reload the page to continue.
error_boundary.heading: Inspira caught it.
errors.backend_unreachable: We couldn't reach Inspira right now. Please check your internet and try again in a moment.
errors.dedupe_failed: We couldn't load merge suggestions. Please try again.
errors.generic: Something went wrong on our end. Please try again.
errors.kickoff_failed: Something went wrong on our end. Please try again in a moment.
errors.outline_failed: We couldn't load the outline. Please try again.
errors.projects_load_failed: We couldn't load your projects. Refresh to try again.
errors.share_link_generate_failed: We couldn't generate a share link. Please try again.
errors.share_link_revoke_failed: We couldn't revoke that share link. Please try again.
errors.summary_failed: We couldn't load the summary. Please try again.
errors.timeline_failed: We couldn't load the timeline. Please try again.
errors.turn_failed: Inspira couldn't finish that turn. Try again.
errors.voice_session_failed: Please try again in a moment. If this keeps happening, check your microphone permissions.
example_banner.body: Play with it, edit freely, or delete it when you’re ready for your own.
example_banner.title: Example project
export.card.csv.label: CSV — spreadsheet friendly
export.card.json.label: JSON — raw structure
export.card.markdown.label: Markdown — edit anywhere
export.card.pdf.label: PDF — printable snapshot
export.docx_description: Edit in Microsoft Word, Google Docs, or Apple Pages.
export.json_description: Raw structured data for your own tools.
export.markdown_description: Plain text with formatting. Works in any editor.
export.pdf_description: A printable document. Opens in any PDF reader.
export.preview.csv: A zip with two spreadsheets — topics.csv and relationships.csv — ready to open in Excel, Numbers, or Google Sheets.
export.preview.json: A single JSON file holding the whole canvas — project, topics, relationships, decisions, and Q&A — keyed under an inspira.canvas.v1 schema tag for future re-import.
export.preview.markdown: One Markdown file that opens cleanly in Obsidian, Notion, or any plain-text editor — topics as headings, decisions as bullets, discussion as blockquotes.
export.preview.pdf: A typeset document laid out like an editorial brief, with every topic, decision, and Q&A turn rendered onto paper-style pages.
export.send_link_description: Generate a read-only link to this project. Anyone with the link can view it; no sign-in needed.
export.txt_description: A simple text file. No formatting.
export_dialog.description: Choose a format to download your project.
export_dialog.format.html.description: A self-contained web page — coming soon.
export_dialog.format.html.title: Read-only HTML
export_dialog.format.json.description: Raw structured data for your own tools — coming soon.
export_dialog.format.json.title: JSON
export_dialog.format.markdown.descriptio: Plain text with formatting — works in any editor.
export_dialog.format.markdown.title: Markdown
export_dialog.format.pdf.description: A printable document with all topics and decisions.
export_dialog.format.pdf.title: PDF
export_dialog.title: Export project
feedback.follow_up_label: Email me if you want to follow up
feedback.message_label: What happened, or what would help?
feedback.message_placeholder: A sentence or two is plenty.
feedback.min_length_error: Please add a little more — at least 10 characters.
feedback.modal_subtitle: A quick note helps shape what lands next. No pressure for a full report.
feedback.modal_title: Tell us what's on your mind
feedback.submit_error: Couldn't send that right now. Please try again.
feedback.success: Thanks — we read every one of these.
home_onboard.1.body: Click any card to jump back in. Inspira remembers where you left off.
home_onboard.1.title: Your projects live here.
home_onboard.2.body: Every new idea gets its own canvas. Map as many as you want.
home_onboard.2.title: Start another.
home_onboard.3.body: Group related projects together. Drag cards between shelves when your thinking evolves.
home_onboard.3.title: Organize with shelves.
import_json_dialog.error_fallback: Something went wrong while importing. Please try again.
import_json_dialog.error_not_json: That file doesn't look like valid JSON.
import_json_dialog.error_read_failed: Couldn't read the file. Try picking it again.
import_json_dialog.error_too_large: That file is too large. Exports over 10 MB aren't supported.
import_json_dialog.error_wrong_schema: Unsupported schema (expected {expected}, got {got}). Only Inspira canvas exports are supported.
import_json_dialog.intro: Pick a JSON file previously exported from an Inspira canvas. We'll recreate the project with its topics, relationships, and decisions.
import_json_dialog.title: Import from JSON
import_json_dialog.title_hint: Useful if you're importing your own export and don't want to overwrite the original's name on the projects list.
import_json_dialog.title_placeholder: Leave blank to keep the original title
inspira.delete.project_consequences: Its topics, decisions, and Q&A are cleared from the canvas.
inspira.delete.topic_consequences: Its Q&A, decisions, and connecting relationships will be cleared.
inspira.kickoff.no_topics_error: The planner didn't return any topics. Try adding one more sentence about what you're trying to make.
kickoff.heading: What do you want to {your_idea}
kickoff.link_prompt: Paste a URL — I'll attach it as context for the planner.
kickoff.markdown_hint: H2 headings become topics. H3 lines become decisions.
kickoff.markdown_placeholder:
# My Project

## Topic One
Some notes about this topic.

### A key decision

## Topic Two
More notes here.
kickoff.paragraph_hint: A paragraph is plenty. You can add more later.
paste_feedback.body: Drop in your full customer-feedback set — one item per line, CSV rows, or a JSON array. Hundreds or thousands of items is fine. Inspira will extract 3-5 recurring themes and auto-generate one project per theme on your home screen, each pre-planned and ready for review.
paste_feedback.error_empty: Paste at least one feedback item to continue.
paste_feedback.error_extract_failed: Theme extraction failed. Try again in a moment.
paste_feedback.error_no_themes: No themes extracted from your feedback. Try a longer or more detailed paste.
paste_feedback.hint: One item per line · or first column of a CSV · or a JSON array · no item-count limit
paste_feedback.placeholder:
Login form is too long
Dashboard is slow on mobile
We need SSO for our enterprise plan
Exports keep timing out
Calendar integration would be huge…
paste_feedback.status_planning: Planning {total} features from your feedback. {done}/{total} ready.
paste_feedback.status_synthesizing: Inspira is reading your feedback to extract recurring themes…
paste_feedback.title: Paste customer feedback
kickoff.placeholder: Describe a feature you want to ship, or paste customer feedback you want triaged. A paragraph is plenty — Inspira will map it into topics, draft the questions, and surface the decisions for you to review.
kickoff.projects_fetch_failed: We couldn't load your projects right now. Try again in a moment.
legal_overlay.h2.privacy.what_we_share: What we share with third parties
legal_overlay.h2.terms.not_allowed: What we ask you not to do
llm_panel.merge_coming_soon: Coming soon — merge not yet implemented
llm_panel.scaffold_failed: The planner couldn't finish the scaffold.
llm_panel.scaffold_upgrade_required: Pro plan required to generate a scaffold.
llm_panel.title: Planner views
maintenance.body: We're making a small change behind the canvas. Back in a few minutes.
maintenance.heading: Inspira is catching its breath.
marketing.about_page.canvas_first.body_1: Lists are good for things you already understand. Canvases are better for things you are trying to understand. When you can see the whole of an idea — the topics it touches, the places it connects, the edges still unclear — you stop re-discovering the same thoughts and start building on them.
marketing.about_page.canvas_first.body_2: Most product tools chose the list. Inspira chose the canvas, and shaped everything else around it.
marketing.about_page.canvas_first.title: Why canvas-first
marketing.about_page.contact.sign_off: We read every message. Response time is usually under a business day.
marketing.about_page.contact.title: Get in touch
marketing.about_page.founder.body_1: I'm building Inspira solo. My background is in B2B software product strategy — I've watched engineering teams drown in customer feedback for years and never get to the work that matters.
marketing.about_page.founder.title: Why I'm building this
marketing.about_page.headline: Built for teams drowning in customer feedback.
marketing.about_page.meta.description: Inspira is a one-person company building the AI teammate that turns customer feedback into ready-to-build features. The triage meeting goes away.
marketing.about_page.meta.title: About Inspira — Built for teams drowning in customer feedback.
marketing.about_page.subhead: Inspira is a one-person company building the AI teammate that turns customer feedback into ready-to-build features. The triage meeting goes away.
marketing.about_page.values.ai_asks.body: Inspira is a good interviewer, not a ghostwriter. I will never add a “write this for me” button. You stay the author; the product stays honest.
marketing.about_page.values.ai_asks.titl: The AI asks. The human answers.
marketing.about_page.values.notebooks.bo: A finished project in Inspira should be a pleasure to scroll through — a record of how you thought, not a grid of what you produced.
marketing.about_page.values.notebooks.ti: Plans should read like notebooks, not spreadsheets.
marketing.about_page.values.title: Values
marketing.about_page.values.unhurried.bo: No meters, no rings, no stage rails, no completion percentages. Progress is what you feel when the canvas fills in.
marketing.about_page.values.yours.body: You can export everything. I do not train on your content. I do not sell your data. A thinking tool that reads your notes has to clear that bar without being asked.
marketing.about_page.values.yours.title: Your work is yours.
marketing.about_page.warm.body_1: The aesthetic is deliberate. Cream paper, serif display type, a soft dotted grid under the canvas, muted sage and gold and rust for accents. It looks like a notebook because it is supposed to feel like one.
marketing.about_page.warm.body_2: Most software about thinking is blue and cold. That language belongs to dashboards — objects you watch, not objects you write in. A thinking tool should feel closer to the page than the screen. The warmth is not decoration; it is a signal that this space is for you, not for a stakeholder waiting on an update.
marketing.about_page.warm.title: Why warm editorial
marketing.features.canvas.body: Drop in a paragraph and watch the idea unfold into connected topics you can drag, link, and revisit. No boxes to tick off, no stages to pass through.
marketing.features.canvas.title: Think on a canvas
marketing.features.heading: Three things Inspira does well.
marketing.features.lede: Not a to-do list. Not a project manager. A place to see the shape of an idea.
marketing.features.questions.body: Inspira asks the right next question at each topic. The more you answer, the sharper the questions get — steering toward what you actually need to decide.
marketing.features.questions.title: Follow-up questions that get sharper
marketing.features.return.body: Every edit, every turn, every decision lives on the canvas. Come back a week later and the thread of your thinking is still right there, ready to continue.
marketing.features.return.title: One click back to your first map
marketing.features_page.cta.line: Try it with feedback you've been sitting on.
marketing.features_page.cta.sub: Free to try. No credit card. Your first canvas opens in under a minute.
marketing.features_page.headline: Inspira, feature by feature.
marketing.features_page.items.f1.body: See the whole feature surface in one glance. Each customer-feedback issue lands as a topic on the canvas — connected to related issues, the projects they spawn, and the decisions made about them. Pan and zoom across the surface; nothing gets buried in a list.
marketing.features_page.items.f1.title: Canvas — the whole feature surface
marketing.features_page.items.f10.body: A dedicated mobile experience ships later. For now Inspira is desktop-first and usable on a large tablet with a keyboard. When mobile lands it'll be a real read-and-review surface — open a canvas on the train, read a topic, speak a thought to Inspira — not a shrunken desktop product.
marketing.features_page.items.f10.title: Mobile — on the way
marketing.features_page.items.f2.body: Click any topic and Inspira asks the questions a thoughtful engineer would ask — what's the constraint, what's the dependency, what's the smallest cut. You answer in plain prose; the canvas captures the decisions. Leave mid-thought and come back.
marketing.features_page.items.f2.title: Topic cards — drill into the details
marketing.features_page.items.f3.body: Nothing you write gets lost. Every answer, every edit, every reshuffle is saved as you go — no save button, no confirmation dialog. Decisions are timestamped and versioned, so you can read back how the thinking moved over a week or a month.
marketing.features_page.items.f3.title: Decisions — kept automatically
marketing.features_page.items.f4.body: Inspira watches the shape of your canvas and occasionally suggests something you haven't added yet — a tradeoff your project hasn't resolved, a related issue from the feedback dump, a constraint you forgot. Suggestions appear as small chips on the canvas edge; accept, dismiss, or ignore.
marketing.features_page.items.f4.title: Suggestions — what's missing
marketing.features_page.items.f5.body: Drop in URLs, upload documents, or paste raw notes onto any card. Inspira keeps the source near the issue it informs and reads it as context. Connect your repo and Inspira pulls in relevant code paths automatically. Sources stay private to your project.
marketing.features_page.items.f5.title: Sources — kept with the work
marketing.features_page.items.f6.body: Inspira exports any project as Markdown for engineers or PDF for everyone else. Both formats include the topics, decisions, and relationships exactly as they sit on the canvas. Nothing is locked in. If Inspira ever disappeared, your work would still be yours.
marketing.features_page.items.f6.title: Export — take your work anywhere
marketing.features_page.items.f7.body: Both light and dark are warm variants of the same notebook. Cream paper, serif type, muted sage and gold accents in light. Deep ink paper, warm cream text, dimmer dotted grid in dark. Inspira remembers your choice and switches cleanly.
marketing.features_page.items.f7.title: Dark mode — warm at night
marketing.features_page.items.f8.body: Run many projects at once — every feature you're shipping has its own canvas. The switcher shows a small preview of each so you remember not just the name of a project but the shape of it. Archive a project to free up the surface; pull it back when it's time.
marketing.features_page.items.f8.title: Multi-project — many features at once
marketing.features_page.items.f9.body: Inspira is single-player today; multi-seat collaboration ships with the autonomous layer. If you want to help build it, open a discussion on GitHub. Until then, every project exports cleanly — sharing your work with a co-builder is as simple as sending a PDF.
marketing.features_page.items.f9.title: Collaboration — on the way
marketing.features_page.items.shelves.bo: Group multiple projects under a shelf and Inspira watches for drift between them. When a decision in one project contradicts another — a different mobile breakpoint, a different auth pattern, a renamed entity — the canvas surfaces it before the inconsistency ships into your codebase.
marketing.features_page.meta.description: From customer feedback to ready-to-build features. The canvas is where Inspira shows its work.
marketing.features_page.meta.title: Features — Inspira
marketing.features_page.subhead: Eleven primitives that turn customer feedback into ready-to-build features and keep them coherent across your codebase.
marketing.hero.subhead: Drop your customer feedback in — Inspira reads it, groups the issues, and scopes the work. Engineering opens the canvas in the morning and starts shipping instead of writing tickets.
marketing.hero.title: Customer feedback in. Inspira ships the work.
marketing.home.hero.illust_aria: Diagram showing customer signals flowing into a canvas of topic cards
marketing.home.hero.subhead: For software teams without a dedicated PM. Inspira reads your customer channels and figures out what to build. Then it generates the code. Your team approves once.
marketing.home.hero.title: From customer feedback to shipped feature. Without the manual triage.
marketing.home.howitworks.heading: How it works.
marketing.home.howitworks.step1.title: Connect your feedback once.
marketing.home.howitworks.step2.body: Sub-agents cluster the signals and decide what ships.
marketing.home.howitworks.step2.title: AI does the thinking while you sleep.
marketing.home.howitworks.step3.body: One canvas to review. Then code ships to your repo.
marketing.home.howitworks.step3.title: Approve once. Code goes to your repo.
marketing.home.meta.description: From customer feedback to shipped feature, without the manual triage. Built for software teams without a dedicated PM.
marketing.home.meta.title: Inspira — From customer feedback to shipped features
marketing.home.personas.eng_manager.body: Your team is 12 engineers with no PM. Customer feedback comes in five channels. You spend Saturday consolidating it into a backlog. Inspira does that on Tuesday morning. You spend Saturday reviewing what shipped.
marketing.home.personas.founder.body: Your support inbox has 200 unread tickets. You're the one triaging every customer signal and you're 8 weeks from your YC interview. Inspira reads the inbox and clusters the signals into themes. Then it decides what to build. You approve the canvas and ship.
marketing.home.personas.founder.title: Technical founder
marketing.home.personas.heading: Built for software teams without a PM.
marketing.home.pricing_teaser.heading: Pricing.
marketing.home.pricing_teaser.sub: Free for solo founders. Paid tiers for teams who want Inspira to triage their feedback autonomously.
marketing.home.values.heading: What Inspira does for your dev team.
marketing.home.values.v1.body: Triage that used to take 4–8 hours of your week happens in 2 minutes. You approve at one canvas, once per project, instead of every customer ticket.
marketing.home.values.v1.title: You stop being the bottleneck.
marketing.home.values.v2.body: Every feature traces back to specific customer voices. "Here are the 12 signals — here's why we shipped these 5 features." To your team and to your investors.
marketing.home.values.v2.title: Your roadmap explains itself.
marketing.home.values.v3.body: Every project has provenance — customer voices and severity baked in. No re-spec, no second-guessing.
marketing.home.values.v3.title: Engineers ship the right features the first time.
marketing.home.what.caption: From customer voice to working code, with one approval in the middle.
marketing.home.what.heading: What Inspira does.
marketing.home.what.shot1_label: See what your team is shipping next.
marketing.home.what.shot3_label: Ship the code, not just the ticket.
marketing.how_it_works.final_cta.heading: Connect your first feedback channel.
marketing.how_it_works.hero.subhead: Three steps. One approval. From customer voice to working code.
marketing.how_it_works.hero.title: Watch your customer feedback become shipped features.
marketing.how_it_works.meta.description: Three steps. One approval. From customer voice to working code.
marketing.how_it_works.meta.title: How it works — Inspira
marketing.how_it_works.step1.body: Inspira reads from your customer-facing channels and treats them as a single feedback stream. Connect your ticketing tools, support inboxes, app-store reviews, and bug trackers. GitHub, Linear, and CSV / JSON paste-in are live today. Intercom, Productboard, Salesforce, and Help Scout are coming for design partners.
marketing.how_it_works.step1.caption: The connectors page — live, design-partner, future.
marketing.how_it_works.step1.label: Step 1
marketing.how_it_works.step1.title: Connect your feedback channels.
marketing.how_it_works.step2.aside: What you don't see: 4 sub-agents reading 247 feedback items, scoring 18 themes by ROI, drafting 12 decisions across 5 topics, resolving 1 conflict between sub-agents, all in 2 minutes.
marketing.how_it_works.step2.body: Inspira clusters thousands of feedback items into themes. The prioritization agent ranks them by ROI, severity-weighted by customer revenue tier. Sub-agents — one per topic — run in parallel. Each drafts decisions with cited sources and flags contradictions inline. The orchestrator reconciles conflicts. You watch from the canvas as decisions populate in real time.
marketing.how_it_works.step2.caption: What you see while Inspira works. Three dots on active topic cards, decisions streaming in as bullets — reasoning one click away.
marketing.how_it_works.step2.label: Step 2
marketing.how_it_works.step2.title: AI does the thinking.
marketing.how_it_works.step3.body: When all sub-agents finish, the orchestrator produces a Decision Summary — every decision, with reasoning and source feedback attached. You approve it or send it back with notes. Once approved, Inspira generates the artifact — working code with a chat sidebar where you can ask for changes. Send to your ticketing tool when ready.
marketing.how_it_works.step3.caption: Decision summary on the left, artifact viewer on the right — one approval in the middle, then code ships.
marketing.how_it_works.step3.label: Step 3
marketing.how_it_works.step3.title: Review the canvas. Ship the code.
marketing.integrations.meta.description: Export, import, and the small integrations that connect Inspira to where you already work.
marketing.integrations.meta.title: Integrations
marketing.landing.autonomous.body: Today Inspira turns the customer feedback you paste in into ready-to-build features. In the next eight weeks the autonomous layer ships: connectors for your repo and feedback channels, a prioritization agent ranking by ROI from your team's data, and Inspira running the loop autonomously — feedback in, features ready for engineering, while your team sleeps. Your team reviews and approves.
marketing.landing.autonomous.cta: Apply to be a design partner →
marketing.landing.autonomous.title: Plug in your repo and feedback streams. Inspira ships features while you sleep.
marketing.landing.band.title: Drop in your customer feedback. Wake up to ready-to-build features.
marketing.landing.howitworks.heading: How it works.
marketing.landing.howitworks.s1.body: Drop in raw feedback — Intercom exports, App Store reviews, sales-call notes, bug reports. Inspira reads through everything; the features and bugs hiding inside come out grouped, ready to action.
marketing.landing.howitworks.s1.title: Customer feedback never gets lost.
marketing.landing.howitworks.s2.body: Each grouped issue lands as a project, scoped and ready for engineering. The triage meeting goes away. Engineering picks up the work and ships.
marketing.landing.howitworks.s2.title: Engineers build instead of gather.
marketing.landing.howitworks.s3.body: Group multiple projects under a shelf and Inspira watches for drift between them. Mobile breakpoints, auth patterns, naming conventions — surfaced before any inconsistency ships into your codebase.
marketing.landing.howitworks.s3.title: Code stays coherent across features.
marketing.landing.meta.description: Drop in your raw customer feedback. Inspira turns it into ready-to-build features so engineering starts each morning shipping, not gathering requirements.
marketing.landing.meta.title: Inspira — From feedback to features.
marketing.landing.preview.caption_body: Engineering picks up where Inspira left off and ships.
marketing.landing.preview.caption_lead: Customer feedback turned into ready-to-build features.
marketing.landing.pricing.sub: Free to try. Pro $49/mo for solo builders. Frontier $200/mo for small eng teams. Enterprise contact pricing when your repo and feedback streams plug in directly.

marketing.pricing.enterprise.name: Enterprise
marketing.pricing.enterprise.price_unit: Pricing scales with your team. Talk to us.
marketing.pricing.enterprise.tagline: For engineering teams shipping autonomously from customer feedback.
marketing.pricing.faq.a1: Your projects and artifacts stay on your workspace. Free-tier caps apply going forward — 5 saved projects, no new artifacts. Past artifacts remain readable. You can re-upgrade any time.
marketing.pricing.faq.a2: Yes. Upgrades pro-rate immediately and you get the new caps right away. Downgrades take effect at the next billing cycle so you keep what you paid for.
marketing.pricing.faq.a3: Yes, on every paid tier. Bring your own Anthropic API key and Inspira routes AI calls through it. Free uses our shared models.
marketing.pricing.faq.a4: An artifact is one Inspira-generated output — a canvas, a code scaffold, or an export. Regenerations count as new artifacts against your monthly cap.
marketing.pricing.faq.a5: Yes. We offer 50% off Pro and Frontier for registered non-profits. Contact the maintainer on GitHub with proof of status and we'll set you up.
marketing.pricing.faq.heading: Frequently asked questions
marketing.pricing.faq.q1: What happens to my data if I downgrade?
marketing.pricing.faq.q3: Is BYOK available on every tier?

marketing.teams.artifact.body: The output of Inspira isn't a ticket or a spec doc. It's working code. After you approve the canvas, Inspira generates the implementation — file tree, code, and tests. A chat sidebar lives next to it where you ask for changes ("add a 100ms debounce to the storage event listener"). When the diff is ready, you accept or reject inline. Send to your repo when ready.
marketing.teams.artifact.caption: File tree on the left, code in the middle, AI chat on the right. Approve or refine inline, then ship.
marketing.teams.artifact.heading: Code is the artifact, not the spec.
marketing.teams.changes.c1.body: The AI scopes implementations from actual customer voice. Your team picks up pre-thought-through tickets — no re-spec, no "let me check what the customer actually said."
marketing.teams.changes.c1.title: Engineers ship the right work the first time.
marketing.teams.changes.c2.body: Every shipped feature traces back to specific customers in specific volumes. You can answer "why this and not that" in one sentence — to your team or your investors.
marketing.teams.changes.c2.title: Your roadmap defends itself.
marketing.teams.changes.c3.body: Triage that took 4–8 hours of your week happens in 2 minutes. Approve a canvas; your team ships.
marketing.teams.changes.c3.title: Saturday is for code, not backlog.
marketing.teams.changes.heading: What changes for your engineering team.
marketing.teams.export.body: Inspira meets your team where they already work. Send a project to your ticketing tool — Decision Summary as the parent issue body, plus sub-issues per topic with priority labels. Send to your repo with checkbox tasks for each sub-issue and links back to the canvas for the team that wants to see the reasoning.
marketing.teams.export.caption: Two export targets, same source: the Decision Summary your team already approved.
marketing.teams.export.heading: Ship to where you already work.
marketing.teams.final_cta.heading: Connect your first feedback channel.
marketing.teams.hero.subhead: For 5–30 person teams without a dedicated product manager. Inspira reads your customer channels and figures out what to build. Then it writes the code. You approve once and your engineers ship.
marketing.teams.hero.title: Run an engineering team without a PM.
marketing.teams.meta.description: Run an engineering team without a PM. Inspira reads your customer channels, figures out what to build, then writes the code.
marketing.teams.meta.title: For software teams — Inspira
marketing.teams.orchestrator.body: When Inspira reads your feedback, an orchestrator agent spawns one sub-agent per topic — running in parallel. Each sub-agent reads the feedback items it owns and drafts decisions with cited sources, flagging contradictions inline. The orchestrator reconciles conflicts between sub-agents and produces the final summary. You watch from the canvas as decisions stream in real time, three loading dots on each active topic card.
marketing.teams.orchestrator.caption: What 4 sub-agents reading 247 feedback items looks like — three dots on active cards, with decisions streaming as bullets and reasoning one click away.
marketing.teams.orchestrator.heading: Inside Inspira: orchestrator + sub-agents.
marketing.teams.pricing_teaser.heading: Pricing.
marketing.teams.pricing_teaser.sub: Free for solo founders. Paid tiers for teams who want Inspira to triage their feedback autonomously.

onboarding.canvas_caption: Each card is a topic. Each dotted line is a real connection between them. Double-click a topic card to open its questions.
onboarding.canvas_caption_short: Every card is a topic. The dotted lines are how they relate. Double-click a topic card to open its questions.

onboarding.first_idea_subtitle: A sentence is enough. A paragraph is plenty.

onboarding.welcome_body: Describe a feature you want to ship, or paste customer feedback you want triaged. Inspira generates the topics, drafts the questions, and surfaces the decisions — you review the draft, edit anything that needs your perspective, and ship.
onboarding.welcome_body_short: Describe a feature, or paste customer feedback. Inspira plans it — you review and ship.

privacy.changes.body: We'll update this page when something material changes, and we'll bump the date at the top so you can tell at a glance. Big changes (who we share data with, what we store) get a notice by email before they take effect.
privacy.children.body: Inspira isn't designed for anyone under 13 (under 16 in the EU). If you think a child has signed up, email us and we'll delete the account.
privacy.cookies.body: We set a session cookie so you stay signed in between visits, plus a small preference cookie that remembers whether you've accepted analytics tracking. If you opt in, we use a privacy-respecting analytics tool to count how many people find Inspira and what pages they land on. That's it — no ad trackers, no third-party pixels. See the cookie banner at the bottom of the page to change your choice any time.
privacy.data_we_collect.body_1: When you sign up we store your email address, a display name if you give us one, and a salted hash of your password. We never store the password itself and we don't have a way to recover it if you forget.
privacy.data_we_collect.body_2: Every canvas you create stays attached to your account: the topics, questions, notes, decisions, and any files or links you attach as sources. We also keep the minimum usage metadata we need to run the product — sign-in timestamps, credit balances, and counters that help us notice abuse.
privacy.h2.changes.summary: We update this page when something material changes.
privacy.h2.children.summary: Inspira is not designed for young children.
privacy.h2.cookies.summary: A session cookie and an optional analytics preference.
privacy.h2.data_we_collect.summary: What we keep when you sign up and use Inspira.
privacy.h2.how_we_use.summary: We use your data to run the product, not to sell it.
privacy.h2.third_parties.summary: A handful of service providers help us run Inspira.
privacy.h2.your_rights.summary: Export, correct, or delete your data from account settings.
privacy.how_we_use.body: We use your data to render your canvas back to you, to send the AI model the prompts you ask it to answer, to bill you if you subscribe, and to reach you about your account (password resets, security notices, service updates). We don't sell anything to anyone, and we don't use your canvas contents to train models without your explicit opt-in.
privacy.intro: Inspira is a warm place to map an idea into connected topics. We want to do that while collecting as little about you as we can get away with. This page tells you what we actually store, who we share it with, and how to get it out of our hands.
privacy.third_parties.cloudflare: sits in front of our servers to block abuse and speed up static file delivery.
privacy.third_parties.fly: runs the Inspira servers that serve the app to your browser.
privacy.third_parties.intro: Running Inspira means handing pieces of your data to a handful of service providers:
privacy.third_parties.neon: (Postgres hosting) — stores your canvases and account data, encrypted at rest.
privacy.third_parties.openai: when you use an AI feature, we send the prompt and the context it needs to the model provider powering that feature. Their terms govern what happens on their end; we pick providers that don't train on API data by default.
privacy.third_parties.stripe: if you pay for a subscription or a credit pack, Stripe processes the payment. We store only the customer identifier we need to match a payment to your account; we never see your card number.
privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.your_rights.body_1: If you're in the EU, UK, or California (and most other places honestly — we treat everyone about the same here) you can ask us for a copy of everything we have about you, correct anything that's wrong, or delete your account entirely. The account settings page has buttons for export and delete, and both take effect immediately. If you'd rather ask a human, email
privacy.your_rights.body_2: Deleted data is removed from our live database right away. Encrypted backups may still hold it for up to 30 days before they rotate out; after that it's gone.
privacy.your_rights.email_address: the operator of your Inspira deployment
projects.archive.empty_body: When you archive a project, it lands here — out of the way, but still around if you want it back.
projects.card.delete_consequences: Its topics, decisions, and Q&A history will be cleared. This cannot be undone.
projects.list.empty_body: Every project starts as a single sentence—a question, a tension, a thing you want to think through. Put that down first; the rest will follow.
projects.list.empty_subtext: A quiet home for the ideas you’re thinking through.
projects.list.title: Your projects.
projects.recently_deleted.delete_consequ: This project will be permanently removed. There's no undo after this.
projects.recently_deleted.empty_body: When you delete a project it lands here for a short grace period — restore it before the window closes, or purge it on the spot.
projects.recently_deleted.subtext: Deleted projects stay here until the grace window closes.
projects.recently_deleted.title: Recently deleted
projects.recently_deleted.toast_restore_: Too late — the grace window has expired.

terms.acceptable_use.body_1: Don't use Inspira to generate or distribute content that infringes someone else's rights, targets children, promotes violence, or breaks the law where you live. Don't try to break into the service, scrape it at scale, or use it to build a competing product by rehosting our output.
terms.acceptable_use.body_2: If you run into a bug, an abusive user, or a prompt-injection attempt, email us and we'll look into it.
terms.accounts.body: Keep your password safe. If someone else gets into your account, tell us right away and we'll help you lock things back down. You can delete your account any time from the account settings page.
terms.changes.body: We'll update this page if something material changes — who runs the service, what it costs, who it's for. The date at the top reflects the most recent revision. If a change is significant we'll email active users before it takes effect.
terms.governing_law.body: These terms are governed by the laws of the State of Delaware, United States, without regard to its conflict-of-laws rules. Any dispute that can't be worked out by email goes to the state or federal courts located in Delaware, and both sides agree that's where the case belongs.
terms.h2.acceptable_use.summary: Don't use Inspira to harm others or break the law.
terms.h2.accounts.summary: Keep your password safe and tell us if it's compromised.
terms.h2.changes.summary: We update if something material changes; email for big shifts.
terms.h2.liability.summary: Inspira is provided as-is, liability capped by law.
terms.h2.our_stuff.summary: The Inspira app, code, and brand belong to us.
terms.h2.subscriptions.summary: Free plans have a credit allowance; paid plans renew monthly.
terms.h2.termination.summary: You can leave any time; we can suspend abusive accounts.
terms.h2.what_inspira_is.summary: A thinking canvas, not financial or legal advice.
terms.h2.your_content.summary: You own what you put into a canvas.
terms.intro: These terms are the agreement between you and the people who run Inspira. If you use the service, you're agreeing to them. We've tried to keep the language honest and short.
terms.liability.body: Inspira is provided as-is. We do our best, but we can't guarantee the service is always available, always accurate, or always the right fit. We're not liable for losses that wouldn't reasonably follow from the service being down, an AI response being wrong, or a third party we rely on having a bad day. To the extent the law lets us cap our liability, we're capping it at what you've paid us in the past twelve months — or $50 if that's more.
terms.our_stuff.body: The Inspira app itself — the code, the brand, the canvas layout, the way the planner asks questions — belongs to us. You can use the app under these terms; you can't rehost it or wrap it in something that makes it look like your own product.
terms.subscriptions.body: Free accounts come with a small credit allowance so you can try Inspira without a card. Paid plans renew monthly (or yearly, if we offer that by the time you're reading this) and bill through Stripe; cancel any time from your account page and we won't charge you for the next period. Credit-pack purchases are non-refundable once the credits land in your balance, but we'll make exceptions for obvious billing mistakes — just ask.
terms.termination.body: You can walk away any time by deleting your account. We can suspend or end an account if it's being used to break these terms, harm other users, or abuse the AI providers we rely on. When we can, we'll email first and explain.
terms.title: Terms
terms.what_inspira_is.body: Inspira is a thinking canvas: you give it an idea, it helps you map that idea into connected topics, and it answers follow-up questions as you explore. It's a tool for thought, not financial, medical, or legal advice. Treat anything an AI feature produces as a draft to review, not a fact to rely on.
terms.your_content.body: Everything you put into a canvas — your idea, your notes, your attached sources — is yours. We take no ownership claim over it. We store it so we can render it back to you and run the AI features you ask us to run; we don't do anything else with it. If you delete a canvas or your account, that's the end of it on our side (subject to the backup-rotation note in the privacy policy).

llm_modes.document.empty_body_fallback: Inspira will generate a long-form document from your project's topics and decisions.
llm_modes.document.empty_body.business_p: Inspira will generate a full business plan from your topics and decisions — covering the things investors and operators look for.
llm_modes.document.empty_body.prd: Inspira will generate a product requirements document from your topics and decisions — problem, customer, goals, requirements, success metrics.
llm_modes.document.empty_body.story_outl: Inspira will generate a story outline from your topics and decisions — characters, world, beats, scenes.
llm_modes.document.empty_body.event_plan: Inspira will generate a full event plan from your topics and decisions — venue, run-of-show, vendors, marketing, day-of staffing.
llm_modes.document.empty_body.marketing_: Inspira will generate a marketing plan from your topics and decisions — positioning, audiences, channels, calendar, budget.
llm_modes.document.empty_body.research_p: Inspira will generate a research proposal from your topics and decisions — questions, methodology, significance, timeline, budget.
llm_modes.document.empty_body.course_out: Inspira will generate a course outline from your topics and decisions — outcomes, modules, materials, schedule, assessment.
llm_modes.document.regenerate_warn_edite: You've edited sections of this document. Regenerate will replace your edits with a fresh draft. Continue?

llm_modes.document.upgrade_body: Upgrade to Pro to draft long-form documents that fit your project type — business plans, PRDs, story outlines, and more.
